name: Sepultura
description: Sepultura is a Brazilian heavy metal band formed in 1984 by brothers Max and Igor Cavalera in Belo Horizonte. One of the most influential metal bands to emerge from Latin America, they pioneered a fusion of thrash and death metal with Brazilian indigenous music and groove-heavy riffing, selling over 20 million records worldwide before concluding with a farewell tour in 2024-2026.
trivia: The name 'Sepultura' is Portuguese for 'grave' or 'sepulture,' inspired by Motorhead's 'Dancing on Your Grave' -- Max Cavalera looked up the translation in a dictionary.
Max Cavalera famously played guitar with only four strings throughout his tenure with the band.
MTV banned the 'Arise' music video due to its apocalyptic religious imagery.
The 'Roots' album featured recordings with the Xavante indigenous people of Brazil, with the band traveling to their reservation in Mato Grosso.
Chaos A.D. was certified Gold by the RIAA, making it one of the few Brazilian metal albums to achieve that distinction.
When auditioning for Max's replacement, candidates included Chuck Billy of Testament and Phil Demmel of Machine Head/Vio-lence.
Derrick Green's first public show with Sepultura was under the pseudonym 'Troops of Doom' at the House of Blues in Los Angeles on July 8, 1998.
